questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She was angry on me for being late.
options: She was angry at me for being late.
She was angry upon me for being late.
She was angry on me for being late.
She was angry with me for being late.
questions.solution: She was angry with me for being late.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The committee were divided in their opinions.
options: The committee has divided in their opinions.
The committee was divided in their opinions.
The committee is divided in its opinions.
The committee were divided in their opinions.
questions.solution: The committee were divided in their opinions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Critical thickness of insulation:
options: The thickness where heat transfer is maximum (for small pipes/wires)
The thickness with minimum heat transfer
questions.solution: Critical radius r_cr = k/h. Below r_cr, adding insulation INCREASES heat transfer.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a vapour compression refrigeration system, the device that reduces pressure is:
questions.solution: Expansion valve: drops pressure from condenser to evaporator.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Flow velocity 5 m/s in a pipe of dia 200 mm gives discharge:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Design for fatigue load:
options: Based on endurance limit Se. Modified by stress concentration, size, surface, reliability
questions.solution: Fatigue: Soderberg (σ_y), Goodman (σ_u), Gerber (σ_u parabolic). ASME elliptic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The air-standard cycle for a compression-ignition (diesel) engine is the:
questions.solution: CI engines approximate the constant-pressure heat-addition Diesel cycle.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Air standard cycle efficiency depends on:
questions.solution: Air standard efficiency ∝ compression ratio (for Otto/Diesel).

questions.stem: Ceramics failure mode is typically:
questions.solution: Ceramics: very brittle. Low fracture toughness. No yielding.

questions.subject: Mechanical Engineering
questions.stem: Parallel axis theorem:
questions.solution: I about any axis = I about parallel centroidal axis + area * distance^2.

questions.solution: RE = h₁ − h₄ (in ideal VCR cycle).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gear train:
options: A series of meshed gears to change speed or torque
questions.solution: Train value = product of driver teeth / product of driven teeth = N_out/N_in.

questions.subject: Mechanical Engineering
questions.stem: Supercharging increases:
questions.solution: Supercharger: increases air mass for more power.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Grinding — wheel specification:
options: Abrasive (Al₂O₃, SiC, CBN, Diamond), Grit size, Grade (A-Z), Structure, Bond (V, R, B, S)
questions.solution: Al₂O₃: steel. SiC: cast iron, carbide. CBN: hardened steel. Diamond: carbide, ceramics.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Tempering of martensite is done to:
questions.solution: Tempering: reduce brittleness of martensite, improve toughness.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cam mechanism converts:
questions.solution: Cam: driver (cam) rotates, follower reciprocates/oscillates. Types: disk, cylindrical, linear.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Virtual work principle:
options: For a system in equilibrium, virtual work of external forces = 0 for virtual displacements
questions.solution: Virtual work: δW = ΣF·δr = 0. Used to solve equilibrium without constraint forces.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kirchhoff's law of thermal radiation:
options: α = ε (absorptivity = emissivity at same wavelength and temperature)
questions.solution: For gray surfaces: α = ε. Absorptivity = emissivity at thermal equilibrium.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pump power:
options: P = ρ·g·Q·H/η. H = total head (static + velocity + friction). η = efficiency
questions.solution: Pump: shaft power = hydraulic power / efficiency. NPSH for cavitation protection.

questions.solution: Pattern: wood, metal, or plastic. Allowances: shrinkage, draft, machining, distortion.

options: Shrinkage, draft, machining, distortion, rapping. Pattern > casting (shrinkage)
questions.solution: Shrinkage: 1-2% (steel). Draft: 1-3° per side. Machining allowance: 1-5 mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Comparing engines between identical temperature limits, which is true?
options: No real engine can exceed the Carnot efficiency
Carnot efficiency depends on the working fluid
A real engine can exceed Carnot if well designed
All engines have the same efficiency
questions.solution: Carnot efficiency is the upper bound and is independent of the working substance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Carnot efficiency depends only on:
questions.solution: η_Carnot = 1 - T_cold/T_hot. All reversible engines have same efficiency between same reservoirs.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Design for static load:
options: Based on σ_y or σ_u. Goodman, Soderberg, Gerber criteria
questions.solution: Static: yielding or fracture. Distortion energy (von Mises) for ductile materials.

questions.solution: Preload: prevents joint separation, improves fatigue life.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: During throttling of a real gas through a valve, the quantity that stays essentially constant is:
questions.solution: Throttling is isenthalpic; the Joule–Thomson effect generally changes temperature.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In an adiabatic process the quantity that is zero is:
questions.solution: Adiabatic ⇒ Q = 0; the gas may still do work and change temperature.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Diesel cycle differs from Otto cycle by:
options: Constant pressure heat addition (vs constant volume)
questions.solution: Otto: constant volume heat addition. Diesel: constant pressure heat addition.

questions.stem: First law of thermodynamics:
options: ΔU = Q - W. Energy cannot be created or destroyed
questions.solution: Closed system. Sign convention: Q in +, W out +. Per unit mass: du = δq - δw.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Otto cycle:
options: Spark ignition. Compression ratio r = V₁/V₂. η = 1 - 1/r^(γ-1)
questions.solution: Otto: 2 isentropic + 2 constant volume. η increases with r. r limited by knock.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For any irreversible process in an isolated system, the total entropy:
questions.solution: The second law: entropy of an isolated system increases for irreversible processes (= 0 only if reversible).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On Mohr's circle for plane stress, the principal stresses are located at:
options: The top and bottom of the circle
The points where the circle crosses the normal-stress axis (shear = 0)
questions.solution: Principal stresses occur where shear stress is zero — the circle's intersections with the σ axis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On a Mohr's circle, the centre is located at:
questions.solution: The centre is at ((σx+σy)/2, 0) on the σ-axis.

questions.stem: S-N curve represents:
options: Stress vs Number of cycles to failure
questions.solution: S-N curve: applied stress amplitude vs number of cycles to failure.

questions.stem: Water flows from a pipe at 307.0 kPa, 3 m/s at elevation 10m to ground level. Neglecting losses, the pressure (kPa) at ground level (velocity 28.6 m/s) is:
questions.solution: Bernoulli: p₂ = p₁ + ½ρ(v₁²−v₂²) + ρg(z₁−z₂) = 0.0000 kPa

questions.solution: Hardness: Brinell (HB), Rockwell (HRC, HRB), Vickers (HV). Related to wear resistance.

options: Increase power output by increasing intake density
questions.solution: Supercharging: increases intake air density → more power.
